CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 2) The proof of service does not show that a copy of the attorney's fee declaration separately filed was served on the requestor of special notice. Probate Code § 2702(a). 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be field: A) In addition to joining in the deed of trust to grant security interest, did the estate assume joint liability to repay the loan under the note or other loan agreements with the lender when the Sherwood Court property was purchased? B) What is the current principal balance of the mortgage on the Sherwood Court property? C) The amount of cash as of a date certain in each account distributable to the former minor must be alleged. D) Is the current bond sufficient? What is the current fair market value of each asset and estimated annual income therefrom?

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP OF PERSON AND ESTATE
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/09/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Statements required by Probate Code § 1064(a)(4)&(5) are missing. 2) Is the current bond sufficient, taking into account the probable annual gross income? 3) Do the petitioners receive account statements in electronic form? If not, have the petitioners made efforts to obtain an original verification letter from each financial institution? See CRC 7.575(b) & LRSF 14.62. 4) Dividend payments shown in the account statements do not match the receipts reported on Schedule A. For example, the 04/01/2018 account statement shows a dividend payment of $0.66 on 04/01/2018, but Schedule A reports the dividend of $0.35 on the same date. 5) All transfers of funds from the trust must be reported on schedules of receipts or distributions. 6) Withdrawals shown in the account statements do not match the disbursements reported on Schedule C. For example, the 04/01/2018 account statement shows two "debit purchases" in the respective amount of $470.59 & $467.14, but Schedule C reports $233.57 & $235.30 on the same date. 7) Original statements of the JP Morgan account listed as "other receipts" on Schedule A showing the balance as of 04/22/2019 & 02/22/2020 must be filed. 8) Original statements of accounts showing the mutual funds described on Schedule E and balance thereof as of 04/22/2019 & 02/22/2020 must be filed. 9) Why did the title company withhold $10,223.04 from the proceeds of the sale of the 62nd Avenue property and then distribute it to the trust? 10) Has a personal representative been appointed for the conservatee's estate? If not, an original small estate affidavit under §§ 13100 et seq. or a certified copy of a spousal property order authorizing the entire distribution to the conservatee's surviving spouse must be on file. See LRSF 14.78(J)(5). 11) The verifications signed by the petitioners need to specifically identify the document. The footer incorrectly refers to "first" accounting.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMAN LLOYD PETERSEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: * ERROR *
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances at https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate ISSUES: 1) An accounting will not be approved until the additional bond is on file. [LRSF 14.40]. 2)In the Supplement page 3 lines 10-12 states the $760.00 paid to George Schofield McCormick LLP legal Services was inadvertently billed and paid prior to the court order. This amount is being deducted from the fee request. The total fee request is now $810.00. There is no authority for payment of any commissions or fees in advance of a court order authorizing such payment, including any retainer paid to an attorney. Where commissions or fees are paid in advance of Court authorization, the Court will ordinarily require an appearance by counsel and a declaration stating the reasons for such payments. [LRSF 14.88].

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/26/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The statements required by CRC 7.703(e)(4)&(5) for the paralegal's fees are missing. 2) CalPERS account statements or copies of uncashed checks verifying the opening balance must be provided. 3) A final account covering the period until the conservatee's death and supplemental account covering the post-death period must be separately provided. Probate Code § 2620(b). An original account statement showing the date-of-death balance must also be filed. 4) Did the petitioner receive any retirement/pension income from the City of San Francisco payable to the conservatee? Why wasn't the retirement/pension plan inventoried as an asset of the conservatorship? 5) Did the petitioner receive any social security income payable to the conservatee? 6) Why did the petitioner use her credit card to pay the disbursement items between 11/4/2019 & 11/29/2019 reported on Schedule C? 7) Did the petitioner obtain court approval to make the two payments after the conservatee's death reported on Schedule C? See § 2631(a). 8) The petitioner's timesheets refer to various payments not reported on Schedule C. Did the petitioner include time she spent on the matters related to and payments made from the trust? 9) How should the cash on hand be allocated between the petitioner and her attorney? 10) The related trust is not under this Court's supervision. Why is the petitioner requesting an order requiring the trustee to pay the fees exceeding the conservatorship cash? See § 2646.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/26/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) As the supplemental declaration (filed 07/29/2020) requests increases of extraordinary compensation, costs, and reserve, such request must be treated as an amendment requiring new notice. The attached proof of service shows that notice was given less than 15 days before the hearing. Also, notice must be given to Alin Beit Noam's attorney. Probate Code § 1214 & CRC 7.51(b). 2) Amnesty International's waiver of account including the name and title of the signing representative has not been filed. 3) The RBC account (ending in 9076) statement shows that the proceeds from the sales of securities were not credited until after the decedent's death. Why didn't the amended inventory show the date-of-death values of those securities? Also, the amended inventory and appraisal lists the account value as of 04/30/2019, but some interest and dividends were paid after the decedent's death. *The petitioner needs to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Dispensing With Request For Special Notice Filed By Camille Colarusso
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 The Motion for Order Dispensing with Request for Special Notice is granted on a showing of good cause, pursuant to Probate Code Sec 1460(e). The opposition to the motion was filed late; however, absent a sufficient showing of prejudice to the moving party, the Court has considered the opposition in reaching its tentative ruling. Under Probate Code section 2700(a), "At any time after the issuance of letters of [ ] conservatorship, [ ] the conservatee, [ ] the spouse or domestic partner of the conservatee, any relative or creditor of the [ ] conservatee, or any other interested person, in person or by attorney, may file with the court clerk a written request for special notice." Based on the papers and supporting declarations, the Court does not find sufficient legal grounds to support Ms. Colarusso's assertion that she is a "relative" of Mr. Sigmund as the term is used in Probate Code section 2700. The Probate Code does not define "relative" as it is used in Probate Code section 2700. However, there is a relevant definition of "relative" under Probate Code section 1821(b), governing notice of a petition for conservatorship: If no spouse or domestic partner of the proposed conservatee or relatives of the proposed conservatee within the second degree are known to the petitioner or proposed conservator, the petition shall set forth, so far as they are known to the petitioner or proposed conservator, the names and addresses of the following persons who, for the purposes of Section 1822, shall all be deemed to be relatives: [?] (4) The natural and adoptive children of the proposed conservatee's siblings. [i.e. the proposed conservatee's natural or adoptive nieces and nephews]. Absent a living spouse or relatives within the second degree of relation to the conservatee, "natural and adoptive" nieces and nephews may be deemed "relatives" for the purpose of giving notice of the proceedings. Although Ms. Colarusso is the daughter of Mr. Sigmund's late spouse's late brother - i.e. Mr. Iveli's "natural and adoptive" niece - the parties do not offer and the Court cannot locate, relevant authority extending the word "relative" as it is used in the Guardianship-Conservatorship Law portion of the Probate Code, and Probate Code section 2700 specifically, to include a proposed conservatee's late spouses' natural or adoptive nieces and nephews.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Dispensing With Request For Special Notice Filed By Camille Colarusso (Additional Line Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 An "interested person" may also request special notice under Probate Code section 2700. The motion is not correct that the definition of "interested person" under Probate Code section 48 cannot apply to conservatorships. Under Probate Code section 20, the definitions in Probate Code section 21 et seq. (including section 48) apply to the entire Probate Code, including the Guardianship-Conservatorship Law. Section 48 defines "interested person" as either: (1) an heir, devisee, child, spouse, creditor, beneficiary, and any other person having a property right in or claim against a trust estate or the estate of a decedent which may be affected by the proceeding; or (2) any person having priority for appointment as personal representative; or (3) a fiduciary representing an interested person. Subsection 48(b) and case law further qualifies the statutory list by providing that an "interested person" may vary from time to time and is to be determined according to the particular facts of the matter. While Probate Code section 1424 gives a specific and limited definition of "interested person" in a conservatorship, whether a person who falls outside of that specific definition is still an "interested party" for purposes of a conservatorship proceeding must be determined on a case-by-case basis, per section 48. The broadness of section 48 conceivably allows special notice to be requested by people who are not also "relatives" to the conservatee as defined by the Probate Code. However, even under the broad definition of "interested person" under Probate Code section 48, based on the papers and declarations, the Court finds an insufficient showing that Ms. Colarusso is an "interested person" in this conservatorship proceeding, under either Probate Code section 48 or 1425. Based on the papers filed with the Court, including the documents filed as exhibits to Ms. Colarusso's opposition, Ms. Colarusso's claimed interest in Mr. Sigmund's conservatorship estate appears to spring from her interest as a creditor and heir to Mr. Thomas Iveli's estate - Mr. Sigmund's late spouse and Ms. Colarusso's late uncle - as well as a claim of interest she believes she will have against Mr. Sigmund's estate after his death, via an agreement between her late father and Mr. Iveli. Based on the papers filed with the Court, any personal relationship between Ms. Colarusso and Mr. Sigmund appears to have been ancillary to Ms. Colarusso's relationship with Mr. Iveli. Without standing as either an "interested person" or a "relative," Ms. Colarusso is not entitled to special notice under Probate Code section 2700. This tentative ruling issues with no effect to the Status Hearing on the Issue of Conflict of Interest Re: Conservator, currently set to be heard on August 12, 2020 at 2:30 p.m. of this court. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 11, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGOT DENISE ANTONETTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/09/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition and proposed orders have been received by the assigned examiner. LRSF 14.6A & 14.93G & K; see also the Court's 6th Amended General Order effective 07/21/2020. 2) The proof of service shows that notice was given before the petition was filed. Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. See LRSF 14.21A. 3) Item 3c of the petition alleges that the decedent had dual German citizenship. Notice must be given to a German Consulate. Probate Code § 8113. 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) What are the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents? B) Item 8 of the petition shows that the two heirs are not residents of the United States. § 8465(d) & (e) factors must be addressed.
